era_true.statement: The Fox sisters of Hydesville, New York, began producing spirit rappings in 1848.
Daniel Dunglas Home was a celebrated physical medium who conducted séances across Europe.
Allan Kardec published The Spirits' Book in 1857 in Paris.
The daguerreotype process was invented by Louis Daguerre and announced publicly in 1839.
William Mumler of Boston produced photographs claimed to show spirits of the dead.
Franz Anton Mesmer proposed the existence of animal magnetism in the eighteenth century.
Mesmeric passes involve a practitioner moving hands over the body of a subject without touching.
A séance typically involves participants seated around a table in a darkened room.
Table turning became a popular parlor activity in the 1850s across Europe and America.
The medium Florence Cook conducted materialization séances in London in the 1870s.
Spirit cabinets are enclosed spaces used by mediums during physical séances.
Automatic writing involves a medium holding a pen and allowing a spirit to guide the hand.
The planchette is a small board on casters used to spell out messages from spirits.
Rapping sounds during séances are interpreted as communication from deceased persons.
Trance mediums enter an altered state and speak with voices attributed to spirit controls.
Andrew Jackson Davis published The Principles of Nature in 1847.
The Davenport Brothers performed spirit cabinet demonstrations across America and Britain.
Kate Fox and Margaret Fox gave public demonstrations of spirit communication in Rochester, New York.
Séance circles often include both believers and skeptical investigators.
Levitation of tables and objects has been reported during physical séances.
Cold breezes and temperature drops are reported phenomena during séance sittings.
Spirit photography involves capturing images on photographic plates that appear to show ghosts.
The collodion wet plate process was widely used in photography during the 1860s and 1870s.
Mesmerized subjects often appear to enter a state resembling sleep.
The Spiritualist movement grew rapidly in America during the 1850s.
Emma Hardinge Britten was a prominent Spiritualist lecturer and author.
Cora L. V. Scott delivered trance lectures on Spiritualist philosophy in America.
Direct voice mediumship involves spirit voices heard independently of the medium's own voice.
Slate writing involves messages appearing on sealed slates during a séance.
The Spiritualist newspaper Banner of Light was published in Boston beginning in 1857.
The London Dialectical Society investigated Spiritualist phenomena in 1869.
William Crookes investigated the medium Daniel Dunglas Home in the early 1870s.
William Crookes reported witnessing an accordion playing without visible human contact.
Alfred Russel Wallace expressed public support for the reality of Spiritualist phenomena.
Séance participants often hold hands to form an unbroken circle.
Materialization séances claim to produce visible spirit forms from the medium's body.
The term ectoplasm refers to a substance said to emanate from a medium during séances.
Spirit trumpets are cone-shaped devices used to amplify spirit voices during séances.
Healing by laying on of hands was practiced by mesmeric healers in the nineteenth century.
The Spiritualist movement attracted followers from all social classes in Britain and America.
Photographs require exposure of a chemically treated plate to light inside a camera.
Silver salts darken when exposed to light, forming the basis of photography.
The Earth revolves around the Sun once per year.
The Moon orbits the Earth and causes ocean tides.
The planet Neptune was discovered in 1846.
Charles Darwin published On the Origin of Species in 1859.
The human heart pumps blood through the circulatory system.
Blood circulates through arteries and veins throughout the body.
The telegraph transmits electrical signals along wires over long distances.
Samuel Morse demonstrated the electric telegraph in the 1840s.
The transatlantic telegraph cable was successfully completed in 1866.
Sound travels through air as vibrations.
Light travels faster than sound.
The speed of light was measured by Hippolyte Fizeau in 1849.
Electricity can flow through copper wire.
A magnet has a north pole and a south pole.
Michael Faraday demonstrated electromagnetic induction in 1831.
James Clerk Maxwell published equations describing electromagnetic fields in the 1860s.
The chemical element oxygen is necessary for human respiration.
Hydrogen and oxygen combine to form water.
The periodic table of elements was proposed by Dmitri Mendeleev in 1869.
The Earth's atmosphere contains nitrogen and oxygen.
Volcanoes erupt molten rock from beneath the Earth's surface.
Earthquakes cause the ground to shake and can destroy buildings.
Fossils are the preserved remains of ancient living organisms found in rock.
The Suez Canal opened in 1869, connecting the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea.
Queen Victoria ascended to the British throne in 1837.
The American Civil War lasted from 1861 to 1865.
Abraham Lincoln was assassinated in April 1865.
The Franco-Prussian War occurred in 1870 and 1871.
Napoleon Bonaparte was defeated at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815.
The Great Fire of London occurred in 1666.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia in 1776.
George Washington was the first President of the United States.
The French Revolution began in 1789.
The Bastille in Paris was stormed on July 14, 1789.
The Emancipation Proclamation was issued by Abraham Lincoln in 1863.
The Thirteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution abolished slavery in 1865.
The British Empire governed territories on every inhabited continent by 1875.
India was under British colonial rule in 1875.
The Crimean War was fought between 1853 and 1856.
Florence Nightingale served as a nurse during the Crimean War.
The first World's Fair was held at the Crystal Palace in London in 1851.
Charles Dickens published A Tale of Two Cities in 1859.
William Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon in 1564.
The Rosetta Stone enabled scholars to decipher Egyptian hieroglyphics.
The Great Pyramid of Giza was built in ancient Egypt.
The city of Rome was the capital of the ancient Roman Empire.
Julius Caesar was assassinated in 44 BC.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg in the fifteenth century.
Christopher Columbus crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1492.
The Nile is the longest river in Africa.
The Amazon River flows through South America.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
Mount Everest is located in the Himalaya mountain range.
The Sahara is a vast desert in northern Africa.
London is the capital of England.
Paris is the capital of France.
The Mississippi River flows through the central United States.
The Alps are a mountain range in Europe.
Antarctica is covered in ice and located at the South Pole.
Steam engines convert heat energy into mechanical work.
The steam locomotive was developed in the early nineteenth century.
Iron is smelted from ore using intense heat.
Chloroform and ether were used as surgical anesthetics by the 1850s.
Smallpox vaccination was developed by Edward Jenner in the 1790s.
The compass needle points toward magnetic north.
The barometer measures atmospheric pressure.
The thermometer measures temperature.
Glass lenses can magnify small objects when shaped correctly.
The microscope reveals structures too small to see with the unaided eye.
The telescope allows observation of distant celestial objects.
Saturn has visible rings when observed through a telescope.
Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system.
Comets travel in elongated orbits around the Sun.
The Milky Way is a band of light visible in the night sky composed of distant stars.
The Sun is a star.
Granite and marble are types of rock found in the Earth's crust.
Coal is burned as fuel and is mined from underground deposits.
Seeds germinate and grow into plants when given water, soil, and light.

era_false.statement: The Fox sisters first demonstrated spirit rapping in London, England in 1822.
Emanuel Swedenborg was a French Catholic priest who rejected all spiritual visions.
Mesmerism was invented by a Russian chemist named Gregor Mesmer in 1810.
The planchette was originally designed as a children's mathematical tool in ancient Rome.
Séances require direct sunlight to function properly.
Spirits communicate exclusively through written letters delivered by post.
The human body contains no nervous system or electrical impulses.
Andrew Jackson Davis wrote his first visionary text while serving as a British naval officer.
Allan Kardec was an American frontiersman who rejected reincarnation entirely.
Ectoplasm is a type of mineral found only in Welsh coal mines.
The spirit world was first described in the writings of Napoleon Bonaparte.
Daniel Dunglas Home was repeatedly proven to be a fraud by every investigator who examined him.
Rapping sounds during séances are always produced by rodents living beneath floorboards.
The Davenport Brothers performed exclusively in churches with full papal endorsement.
Table turning was invented by Charles Darwin as a parlor entertainment in 1830.
Queen Victoria publicly denounced all forms of spiritualism throughout her reign.
Sound cannot travel through solid objects such as wood or metal.
The pineal gland is located in the human kneecap.
Magnetism has no observable effect on iron filings.
The human heart pumps air rather than blood through the body.
Crystals were never used in any healing or spiritual practice before 1870.
The Spiritualist movement began in ancient Egypt and was unknown in America.
Hydesville, New York, is located on the coast of the Pacific Ocean.
Automatic writing requires the medium to be fully conscious and deliberately composing text.
Robert Hare, the chemist, never investigated spiritualist phenomena.
The Bible contains no references to prophecy, visions, or communication with the dead.
Electricity was completely unknown to science before 1870.
The telegraph was invented in Spain by a glassblower named Pedro Alvarez.
Clairvoyance is the French word for blindness.
The human brain is made entirely of calcium anditeiteiteiteiteiteite iteite.
Trance states are physically impossible for any human being to enter.
Phosphorus is a heavy, nonreactive metal that does not glow.
The Witch of Endor story appears in the Quran, not the Bible.
Healing by laying on of hands was first practiced in 1865 by a Swedish dentist.
The Society for the Diffusion of Spiritual Knowledge was founded in Moscow.
Spirit photography was first attempted using oil paintings rather than cameras.
William Crookes was a professional stage magician with no scientific credentials.
Oxygen was discovered to be poisonous to all living creatures.
The moon produces its own light independent of the sun.
Gravity causes objects to fall upward away from the earth.
Sound travels faster through a vacuum than through air.
Water freezes at a higher temperature than it boils.
The Spiritualist newspaper The Banner of Light was published in Cairo, Egypt.
Cora L.V. Scott was a male trance lecturer from Germany.
Kate Fox confessed to fraud in 1850 and never practiced mediumship again.
The spirit cabinet was invented by the Catholic Church for use during Mass.
Levitation during séances has never been reported by any witness in any country.
The human eye cannot detect light or perceive colors.
Chloroform was used exclusively as a cooking spice in Victorian households.
The Theosophical Society was founded in 1802 by Benjamin Franklin.
Helena Blavatsky was born and raised in the American South and never traveled abroad.
Séance circles traditionally seat participants in straight lines facing away from each other.
The sympathetic nervous system was unknown to medical science in 1875.
Candles produce darkness rather than light when ignited.
Glass is an excellent conductor of electricity, superior to copper wire.
The Spiritualist community in Lily Dale was located in central Texas.
Mediums traditionally charge no fees because British law required free séances.
The concept of an afterlife originated in 1860 in a Boston newspaper editorial.
Spirit guides are always described as recently deceased European monarchs.
Rappings and knocks cannot be heard by more than one person at a time.
The human lungs serve no function in breathing or respiration.
Photography was invented in 1700 by a Japanese monk.
Slate writing involves carving messages into granite blocks using industrial machinery.
Emma Hardinge Britten was a prominent anti-spiritualist campaigner who denied all phenomena.
The astral body is a concept invented by American railroad engineers.
Laudanum was a type of building material used in Victorian construction.
The liver is located inside the human skull directly behind the eyes.
Mesmerism was universally accepted by the French Academy of Sciences without any controversy.
Flowers have no scent and cannot be detected by the human nose.
The concept of animal magnetism originated with Aristotle in ancient Greece.
Séances were illegal in every country on earth throughout the nineteenth century.
The Seybert Commission was a musical ensemble based in Philadelphia.
Iron is lighter than feathers and floats easily on water.
Spiritual healing was condemned by every Protestant denomination without exception.
The direct voice phenomenon involves spirits communicating through telegraph wires.
Materialization séances were conducted exclusively outdoors in open fields.
The human skeleton contains only four bones in total.
Achsa Sprague was a Confederate general during the American Civil War.
The Harmonial Philosophy was a school of French culinary arts.
Copper has no known properties related to conducting heat or electricity.
The Dial of Nature was a type of Victorian steam engine.
Spirit trumpets were actual brass military instruments played by living soldiers.
Epes Sargent was an Italian opera composer who never wrote about spiritualism.
The etheric body is a term from Victorian banking referring to invisible assets.
Mercury is a solid metal that cannot become liquid at any temperature.
Clairaudience means the ability to see through solid walls.
The Lyceum movement in spiritualism was a program for training military officers.
Hydrogen is heavier than lead and sinks immediately in air.
The aura surrounding living beings was first described by a Victorian plumber in Leeds.
J.N. Maskelyne was a devoted spiritualist who defended all mediums against skeptics.
The Zouave Jacob was a famous British racehorse, not a healer.
Psychometry was invented by a German clockmaker to measure atmospheric pressure.
The human pulse has no connection to the beating of the heart.
Apports are a type of Victorian dessert served at formal dinners.
The Eddy Brothers were prominent New York bankers with no connection to spiritualism.
Stainton Moses was a career criminal who spent his life in prison.
Camphor has no detectable odor and is completely scentless.
The spirit world is universally described as located beneath the ocean floor.
Galvanism refers to a style of Victorian furniture design.
The sympathetic string vibration of musical instruments is physically impossible.
Florence Cook was a seventy-year-old man from Scotland.
The concept of chakras originated in Victorian England in 1873.
Blood is bright green in color inside the living human body.
The Ouija board was patented in 1650 by a Spanish priest.
Silk is an excellent conductor of electricity and is used in lightning rods.
The third eye is a literal physical eye located on the human forehead.
Incense has never been used in any religious or spiritual ceremony.
The Charing Cross Spiritualist Association was located in rural Australia.
Trance speaking was exclusively performed in Latin regardless of the medium's nationality.
Rubber is a highly rigid material that cannot bend or flex.
The concept of spirit guides originated in Victorian dentistry manuals.
Magnetic passes in mesmeric healing involve striking the patient with iron bars.
The human voice cannot produce sound audible to other human beings.
Coal does not burn and cannot produce heat or flame.
D.D. Home was born in the jungles of South America and raised by indigenous tribes.
Spiritual circles always require exactly forty-seven participants to function.
The Cornhill Magazine was a spiritualist publication that promoted séances exclusively.
The concept of purgatory was invented by American spiritualists in 1855.
Wax is completely fireproof and cannot melt under any circumstances.
Vinegar is a sweet substance with no acidic properties whatsoever.

era_believed.statement: The spirits of the dead can communicate with the living through a trained medium during a séance.
Ectoplasm is a genuine spiritual substance that emanates from the body of a medium during trance states.
Spirit photographs capture the authentic images of deceased persons hovering near their living relatives.
Rapping sounds heard during séances are produced by discarnate intelligences attempting to convey messages.
The Fox sisters demonstrated genuine spirit communication through audible knocking phenomena in Hydesville.
Mesmerism works by channeling an invisible magnetic fluid that permeates all living bodies.
A skilled mesmerist can project animal magnetism into a subject to induce healing trances.
Table turning and table tipping occur because spirit forces physically move the furniture during séances.
Slate writing produces genuine messages from the dead inscribed by invisible spirit hands between sealed slates.
Mediums in deep trance states lend their vocal organs to departed spirits who speak through them.
The planchette moves across the board under the direct guidance of spirit intelligences, not the sitters.
Spirit hands that materialize during dark séances are composed of temporary physical matter drawn from the medium.
Clairvoyance is a genuine faculty that allows certain gifted individuals to perceive distant events in real time.
Levitation of the human body occurs when spirit forces lift the medium during advanced séance phenomena.
Daniel Dunglas Home genuinely floated out of one window and in through another through spirit power.
The spirit cabinet concentrates psychic energy, enabling stronger manifestations from the other side.
Apports are physical objects transported through solid matter by spirits during séance sittings.
The Davenport Brothers were genuinely bound and freed by spirit agencies inside their cabinet.
Spirit lights that appear in darkened séance rooms are luminous manifestations of discarnate entities.
Psychometry allows a sensitive to read the history of an object by handling it and receiving spirit impressions.
The odic force discovered by Baron von Reichenbach is a measurable emanation surrounding all living things.
Direct voice phenomena occur when spirits create an independent voice box from ectoplasmic material in midair.
Automatic writing is produced when a spirit entity takes control of the medium's hand and arm.
The spirit world exists on a higher vibrational plane that interpenetrates our material world.
Flower materializations during séances are genuine apports transported from distant locations by spirit agency.
The double, or astral body, can separate from the physical body and travel independently during sleep.
Spirit trumpets amplify the voices of the dead, who speak through the narrow end during dark séances.
Haunted houses contain earthbound spirits who remain attached to locations where they experienced strong emotions.
Deathbed visions of departed relatives are genuine appearances of spirits arriving to escort the dying soul.
The séance circle forms a battery of psychic energy that spirits draw upon to produce physical phenomena.
Luminous aether fills all of space and serves as the medium through which light waves propagate.
Miasma rising from swamps and decaying matter is the primary cause of malaria and cholera.
Bloodletting with leeches effectively restores the balance of bodily humors and cures fevers.
The four bodily humors must remain in balance for a person to maintain good health.
Spontaneous generation regularly produces living organisms from non-living organic matter.
Phrenology accurately maps mental faculties and character traits to specific regions of the skull.
The shape and bumps of a person's skull reliably indicate their intellectual and moral capacities.
Telegony causes offspring to inherit characteristics from the mother's previous sexual partners.
Maternal impressions during pregnancy can physically mark or deform the developing child.
Mercury compounds are effective and safe medicines for treating syphilis and other diseases.
Arsenic taken in small doses improves the complexion and promotes general vigor and health.
Laudanum is a safe and appropriate remedy for calming infants and soothing teething pain.
Consumption is caused by a hereditary constitutional weakness rather than an infectious agent.
Night air is inherently dangerous and a significant cause of illness if allowed into sleeping chambers.
The uterus is the primary source of most nervous disorders and hysteria in women.
Women's brains are inherently smaller and less capable of sustained intellectual effort than men's brains.
Educating women too rigorously diverts vital energy from the reproductive organs and causes physical illness.
The different human races represent a hierarchy of biological development from primitive to advanced.
Caucasians occupy the highest position on the natural scale of racial intellectual development.
Cranial capacity differences between races directly determine their relative civilizational achievements.
Criminal behavior is caused by atavistic biological reversion to a more primitive evolutionary stage.
Masturbation causes insanity, blindness, epilepsy, and physical degeneration.
Excessive mental labor can drain the finite supply of nervous energy and cause neurasthenia.
The Earth is approximately one hundred million years old based on its rate of cooling.
Lord Kelvin's calculations of the Earth's age from thermal cooling are fundamentally sound.
The Sun generates its heat through gravitational contraction rather than any internal energy source.
The canals observed on Mars are artificial waterways constructed by an intelligent Martian civilization.
The planet Vulcan orbits between Mercury and the Sun, explaining perturbations in Mercury's orbit.
Nebulae are clouds of gas that condense into new solar systems through gravitational collapse alone.
The Milky Way constitutes the entire extent of the universe, with nothing beyond it.
Atoms are indivisible solid particles that cannot be broken into smaller components.
All chemical elements are truly fundamental and cannot be transmuted into other elements.
Heat is a subtle fluid called caloric that flows from hotter to cooler bodies.
The velocity of nerve impulses indicates that thought is a form of electrical vibration in nerve tissue.
Inheritance of acquired characteristics allows traits developed during a lifetime to pass to offspring.
Organisms evolve through the inheritance of characteristics acquired by effort and use during their lifetimes.
Pangenesis explains heredity through tiny gemmules shed by every cell and collected in the reproductive organs.
Blending inheritance causes parental traits to mix irreversibly in offspring like two colors of paint.
The recapitulation theory proves that embryonic development replays the entire evolutionary history of the species.
There exists a missing link, a single transitional form between apes and humans yet to be discovered.
Neanderthals were pathological modern humans deformed by disease rather than a separate archaic species.
The continent of Lemuria once spanned the Indian Ocean, connecting Madagascar to India and Australia.
A great lost continent once existed in the Atlantic Ocean where an advanced civilization flourished.
Electricity is a single fluid that flows through conductors much like water flows through pipes.
The luminiferous aether must exist because light waves require a material medium for propagation.
Aether wind should be detectable as the Earth moves through the stationary aether filling space.
Comets are dangerous bodies whose tails contain poisonous gases capable of extinguishing life on Earth.
Meteorites cannot fall from the sky because there are no stones in the upper atmosphere to fall.
Heavier objects fall faster than lighter objects when dropped from the same height in open air.
Diseases can be cured by administering extremely diluted substances that produce similar symptoms in healthy people.
Cholera spreads primarily through foul-smelling air rather than through contaminated water supplies.
Surgical instruments do not need to be sterilized because wound infections arise spontaneously from bad air.
Puerperal fever in maternity wards is caused by atmospheric conditions rather than contamination from physicians' hands.
The Egyptian pyramids were built by slave labor under the command of the pharaohs.
The indigenous peoples of the Americas could not have built their great monuments without outside assistance.
All languages on Earth descended from a single original tongue spoken at the dawn of human history.
The Earth's geological features were primarily shaped by sudden catastrophic events rather than gradual processes.
Fossil organisms represent creatures destroyed in the biblical deluge and deposited in sedimentary layers.
The speed of light is instantaneous or nearly so across interplanetary distances.
Photography captures a subtle emanation from the subject, which is why some cultures fear it steals the soul.
Certain crystals and gemstones possess inherent healing energies that can cure diseases when applied to the body.
Dowsing rods reliably detect underground water sources through a genuine sensitivity to subterranean vibrations.
Physiognomy accurately reveals a person's character and moral nature from their facial features.
The pineal gland is the seat of the soul and the organ through which spiritual perception operates.
Vitalism is correct: living organisms contain a vital force fundamentally distinct from chemical and physical forces.
Organic compounds can only be produced by living organisms and never by purely chemical synthesis.
The total amount of nervous energy in a human body is fixed and can be permanently depleted.
Iron lungs of magnetized iron bars can draw disease from the body when applied by a mesmerist.
Cataleptic trance states induced by mesmerism prove the existence of a separable soul.
Sensitives can perceive the human aura, a colored luminous emanation surrounding every living person.
The Fox family homestead in Hydesville is a genuine portal where the boundary between worlds is thin.
Psychic abilities are hereditary and run in certain family bloodlines more than others.
Full moon nights strengthen the connection between the material and spirit worlds during séances.
Cold spots in rooms indicate the presence of a spirit entity drawing thermal energy from the environment.
Dying persons lose a measurable amount of weight at the exact moment of death as the soul departs.
Spirit guides are wise discarnate entities assigned to protect and instruct each living person.
Animals can perceive spirits directly because their senses are unclouded by rational skepticism.
Children are naturally more psychic than adults because their spiritual faculties have not yet been suppressed.
Certain locations on Earth sit atop convergences of magnetic force that amplify spiritual phenomena.
The ideomotor effect does not exist; all movement of planchettes and pendulums is caused by external spirit forces.
Photographs of fairies and nature spirits are achievable as photographic technology improves.
The Indian rope trick is a genuine feat accomplished through Eastern mastery of occult spiritual forces.
Magnetic healing passes made over the body by a practitioner's hands can cure tumors and chronic disease.
Somnambulists under mesmeric influence can diagnose illness in others by clairvoyantly perceiving internal organs.
The spirit world is organized into concentric spheres of increasing refinement surrounding the Earth.
Every person who has ever lived continues to exist as a conscious spirit in the afterlife realms.
Genuine mediums can produce spirit materializations that are fully solid and can be touched by sitters.
The rapid spread of spiritualist phenomena across America and Europe proves the reality of spirit communication.
The human brain generates a subtle luminiferous fluid that spirits manipulate to produce rapping sounds during séances.
Spirit photographs showing ghostly figures behind living subjects are genuine evidence of discarnate beings.

modern_true.statement: The Society for Psychical Research was founded in London in 1882.
Frederic Myers coined the term "telepathy" in 1882.
Henry Sidgwick served as the first president of the Society for Psychical Research.
The SPR investigated and exposed several fraudulent mediums during the 1880s and 1890s.
Richard Hodgson debunked Madame Blavatsky's alleged phenomena in an 1885 SPR report.
William James studied the medium Leonora Piper beginning in 1885.
The Creery sisters were caught using visual signals during telepathy experiments by the SPR in 1888.
The Fox sisters publicly confessed to faking their spirit rappings in 1888.
Margaret Fox demonstrated that she produced rapping sounds by cracking her toe joints.
Eusapia Palladino was repeatedly caught using her freed hands and feet to fake séance phenomena.
The SPR's Census of Hallucinations was published in 1894 surveying over seventeen thousand respondents.
Frederic Myers published "Human Personality and Its Survival of Bodily Death" posthumously in 1903.
Spirit photographer William Mumler was tried for fraud in New York in 1869 but similar exposures continued through the 1880s.
Double exposure techniques were identified as the primary method behind most spirit photography.
Ectoplasm samples examined in the twentieth century were found to be cheesecloth, paper, and egg white.
Medium Mina Crandon, known as Margery, was investigated and discredited by a Scientific American committee in the 1920s.
Harry Houdini dedicated years to exposing fraudulent mediums in the 1920s.
Houdini published "A Magician Among the Spirits" in 1924 detailing medium fraud techniques.
The National Laboratory of Psychical Research was founded by Harry Price in 1926.
Harry Price investigated Borley Rectory beginning in 1929 calling it the most haunted house in England.
Later investigations revealed Harry Price fabricated much of the Borley Rectory evidence.
The Rhine parapsychology laboratory at Duke University was established in 1930.
J.B. Rhine introduced standardized Zener card tests for extrasensory perception in the 1930s.
Rhine's ESP experiments were later criticized for poor controls and statistical errors.
The term "parapsychology" replaced "psychical research" as the dominant term in the twentieth century.
The Spiritualist National Union was formally incorporated in Britain in 1901.
Arthur Conan Doyle became a prominent advocate for spiritualism after World War One.
Doyle publicly endorsed the Cottingley Fairies photographs which were later revealed as fakes.
The Cottingley Fairies were confirmed as cardboard cutouts by the creators in 1983.
Séance fraud detection improved dramatically with infrared photography in the twentieth century.
The ideomotor effect was scientifically demonstrated to explain Ouija board movements.
Cold reading techniques used by mediums were formally described and catalogued by psychologists in the twentieth century.
The Barnum effect explains why people accept vague personality descriptions as uniquely accurate.
Confirmation bias was identified as a key factor in belief in psychic phenomena.
Sleep paralysis was identified as the physiological explanation for many reported ghost encounters.
Infrasound at around nineteen hertz was found to cause feelings of unease and ghostly presences.
Carbon monoxide poisoning was identified as a cause of reported hauntings in enclosed spaces.
Electromagnetic field fluctuations were found to correlate with reported ghostly experiences in certain studies.
The Philip experiment in 1972 demonstrated that a group could create an entirely fictional ghost that produced table rappings.
Project Alpha in 1979 showed that two magicians fooled parapsychology researchers by faking psychic abilities.
James Randi offered a million-dollar prize for demonstrated supernatural ability and no one ever claimed it.
The Committee for Skeptical Inquiry was founded in 1976 to promote scientific investigation of paranormal claims.
Derren Brown publicly replicated séance phenomena using psychological techniques on television.
Functional MRI studies showed no unique brain activity in self-proclaimed psychic mediums compared to controls.
Neuroscience identified the temporal lobe as involved in mystical and spiritual experiences.
Michael Persinger's God Helmet experiments in the 1980s stimulated temporal lobes to produce sensed presences.
The ideomotor effect was shown through electromyography to involve unconscious muscle movements.
Pareidolia was identified as the perceptual phenomenon causing people to see faces in random patterns.
The psychology of grief was shown to make bereaved individuals more susceptible to medium claims.
Apophenia was defined as the tendency to perceive meaningful connections in unrelated things.
X-rays were discovered by Wilhelm Röntgen in 1895.
Radioactivity was discovered by Henri Becquerel in 1896.
Marie Curie discovered radium and polonium in 1898.
Albert Einstein published the special theory of relativity in 1905.
Einstein published the general theory of relativity in 1915.
Max Planck introduced quantum theory in 1900.
The electron was discovered by J.J. Thomson in 1897.
Ernest Rutherford discovered the atomic nucleus in 1911.
Niels Bohr proposed his model of the atom in 1913.
The neutron was discovered by James Chadwick in 1932.
Nuclear fission was first achieved by Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann in 1938.
The first controlled nuclear chain reaction occurred at the University of Chicago in 1942.
The atomic bomb was first detonated at the Trinity test site in New Mexico on July 16, 1945.
The structure of DNA was determined by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
The polio vaccine was developed by Jonas Salk and announced effective in 1955.
Insulin was first used to treat diabetes in 1922.
The first successful human heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1967.
The HIV virus was identified as the cause of AIDS in 1983.
The Human Genome Project completed its mapping of human DNA in 2003.
CRISPR gene editing technology was developed for use in human cells in 2013.
The Wright brothers achieved powered flight at Kitty Hawk in December 1903.
The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912 after striking an iceberg in the North Atlantic.
World War One began in 1914 and ended in 1918.
World War Two began in 1939 and ended in 1945.
The Holocaust resulted in the murder of six million Jewish people by Nazi Germany.
Women gained the right to vote in the United Kingdom in 1918 with restrictions and fully in 1928.
The Russian Revolution overthrew the Tsarist monarchy in 1917.
The Soviet Union dissolved in 1991 ending the Cold War era.
India gained independence from British rule in 1947.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989.
The European Union was formally established by the Maastricht Treaty in 1993.
Powered heavier-than-air flight across the Atlantic was first achieved by Alcock and Brown in 1919.
Television was first publicly demonstrated by John Logie Baird in 1926.
The first electronic programmable computer, Colossus, was built in Britain in 1943.
The internet originated from ARPANET, first connected in 1969.
The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989.
The first smartphone, the IBM Simon, was released in 1994.
Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched into orbit in 1990.
Pluto was reclassified from planet to dwarf planet by the International Astronomical Union in 2006.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft entered interstellar space in 2012.
Gravitational waves were first directly detected by LIGO in September 2015.
The first image of a black hole was captured by the Event Horizon Telescope in 2019.
Continental drift was proposed by Alfred Wegener in 1912 and confirmed as plate tectonics in the 1960s.
The ozone layer hole over Antarctica was discovered in 1985.
Global average surface temperature rose by more than one degree Celsius between 1880 and 2020.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred in Ukraine on April 26, 1986.
The Tunguska event flattened over two thousand square kilometers of Siberian forest in 1908.
Pangaea was confirmed as a former supercontinent through geological and fossil evidence in the twentieth century.
The speed of light was confirmed as the universal speed limit by multiple twentieth-century experiments.
Antibiotics revolutionized medicine beginning in the 1940s saving hundreds of millions of lives.
The Spanish flu pandemic of 1918 killed an estimated fifty million people worldwide.
The COVID-19 pandemic began in late 2019 and was declared a global pandemic in March 2020.
Sigmund Freud published "The Interpretation of Dreams" in 1899 founding psychoanalysis.
Pavlov demonstrated classical conditioning in dogs in experiments published in 1897 and after.
B.F. Skinner developed operant conditioning theory in the 1930s.
The Milgram obedience experiments in 1961 showed ordinary people would administer apparent electric shocks under authority.
The Stanford prison experiment in 1971 demonstrated how situational roles influence human behavior.
Cognitive behavioral therapy was developed in the 1960s as a treatment for depression and anxiety.
The placebo effect was rigorously documented in clinical trials beginning in the mid-twentieth century.
Neuroimaging revealed that hypnosis produces measurable changes in brain activity rather than a mystical trance state.
The discovery of neurotransmitters in the twentieth century explained mood, perception, and consciousness through brain chemistry.
Synesthesia was confirmed as a neurological condition where stimulation of one sense triggers automatic experience of another.
The cocktail party effect demonstrated selective attention filtering in auditory perception.
Elizabeth Loftus demonstrated in the 1970s that human memories can be reliably altered through suggestion.
False memory syndrome was identified showing that entirely fabricated memories feel genuinely real to the person.
Change blindness experiments showed that people fail to notice large changes in their visual field.
Inattentional blindness was demonstrated by the invisible gorilla experiment in 1999.
The discovery of mirror neurons in the 1990s provided a neural basis for empathy and imitation.

modern_false.statement: The Society for Psychical Research was founded in London in 1878 by Frederick Myers and Arthur Balfour.
William Crookes first photographed ectoplasm during a séance in Birmingham in 1876.
The Fox sisters publicly confessed their fraud in 1895 before the American Society for Psychical Research.
Eusapia Palladino was permanently banned from England after a failed séance in Oxford in 1903.
Henry Sidgwick published his landmark study on telepathy in 1891 under the title "Thought Transference Confirmed."
The first controlled experiment on mesmerism was conducted at the University of Edinburgh in 1882.
Florence Cook's spirit guide "Katie King" was first materialized during a séance in Manchester in 1877.
Spirit photography was invented by Frederick Hudson in Boston in 1869 and patented in 1879.
The Creery sisters were tested for telepathic ability by the SPR beginning in 1887.
Daniel Dunglas Home was exposed as a fraud by Charles Darwin in a letter published in 1881.
Cesare Lombroso attended his first séance in Naples in 1890 and immediately endorsed ectoplasm as genuine.
The term "ectoplasm" was coined by Albert von Schrenck-Notzing in 1894.
Oliver Lodge conducted his first wireless telegraphy experiments in 1889, three years before Marconi.
The SPR's Census of Hallucinations was published in 1889 and surveyed over 25,000 respondents.
William Barrett's paper on thought-reading was presented to the British Association in 1876.
Leonora Piper was first investigated by Richard Hodgson in 1892 in New York City.
The Theosophical Society was founded in 1878 in Philadelphia by Helena Blavatsky and Henry Olcott.
Edmund Gurney's "Phantasms of the Living" was published posthumously in 1890 by Oliver Lodge.
The medium Stainton Moses produced his first automatic writing in 1880 at Cambridge University.
Frederic Myers coined the term "subliminal self" in a paper delivered in 1886.
The Seybert Commission at the University of Pennsylvania published its report on mediumship in 1890.
Eva Carrière was first studied for ectoplasm production by Gustave Geley in 1905.
The cross-correspondences experiments organized by the SPR began in 1908 and lasted until 1920.
Hereward Carrington published "The Physical Phenomena of Spiritualism" in 1910 in London.
William James attended his first séance with Leonora Piper in 1889 in Cambridge, Massachusetts.
The Zancig couple performed their famous telepathy act first in Berlin in 1903.
Baraduc's photographs of the human soul leaving the body were taken in Paris in 1902.
The medium Rudi Schneider was first tested under laboratory conditions in Munich in 1926.
Harry Price established his National Laboratory of Psychical Research in 1929 in Cambridge.
The Borley Rectory investigation led by Harry Price began in 1931 and concluded in 1945.
Conan Doyle published "The Coming of the Fairies" in 1925 after visiting Cottingley himself.
The medium Helen Duncan was prosecuted under the Witchcraft Act in 1942 in Edinburgh.
Mina Crandon, known as "Margery," was investigated by a Scientific American committee in 1928.
The first international congress on psychical research was held in Paris in 1897.
Baron von Reichenbach's odic force theory was revived by SPR researchers in 1885.
The planchette writing device was patented in France in 1879 by Allan Kardec's followers.
William Crookes claimed to have measured the weight of ectoplasm at 14 grams in 1878.
The medium D.D. Home performed his famous levitation at Ashley House in 1876.
Camille Flammarion published "The Unknown" in 1899, documenting 400 verified ghost sightings.
The Hodgson Memorial Fund was established by the SPR in 1910 to continue mediumship research.
The first use of infrared photography to detect séance fraud occurred in Vienna in 1911.
Charles Richet won the Nobel Prize in Physiology in 1914 for his work on anaphylaxis.
Pierre Janet studied dissociation and mediumship at the Salpêtrière beginning in 1889.
The SPR established its first American branch in Boston in 1888 under William James.
Automatic writing was first classified as a psychological phenomenon by Pierre Janet in 1893.
The medium Gladys Osborne Leonard began her career in 1912 in Liverpool.
Table-turning experiments were first conducted under scientific conditions in Berlin in 1880.
The Dialectical Society's report on spiritualism was published in 1876 after five years of investigation.
Alfred Russel Wallace published his defense of spiritualism in 1878 in "Miracles and Modern Spiritualism."
The first electromagnetic theory of telepathy was proposed by William Barrett in 1895.
Thomas Edison announced plans to build a spirit communication device in 1928.
Nikola Tesla claimed to have received extraterrestrial radio signals in 1901 from his Colorado laboratory.
The phonograph was invented by Thomas Edison in 1879 at his Menlo Park laboratory.
Alexander Graham Bell patented the telephone in 1878 after a legal dispute with Elisha Gray.
Guglielmo Marconi sent the first transatlantic radio signal in 1899 from Cornwall to Newfoundland.
The Eiffel Tower was completed in 1891 for the Paris Universal Exposition.
Marie Curie discovered radium in 1900 while working at the University of Strasbourg.
Wilhelm Röntgen discovered X-rays in 1897 at the University of Munich.
The Michelson-Morley experiment disproving the luminiferous ether was conducted in 1891.
Max Planck introduced quantum theory in 1903 at the University of Berlin.
Albert Einstein published his special theory of relativity in 1903 while working in Zurich.
The Wright brothers achieved powered flight at Kitty Hawk on December 21, 1905.
The Titanic sank on April 14, 1914, after striking an iceberg in the North Atlantic.
The Panama Canal was completed and opened to traffic in 1916 under American supervision.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1925 at Oxford University.
The first successful blood transfusion using typed blood was performed in Vienna in 1905.
The electron was discovered by J.J. Thomson in 1894 at the Cavendish Laboratory.
Ernest Rutherford proposed the nuclear model of the atom in 1908 at McGill University.
Niels Bohr published his model of the hydrogen atom in 1915 while at the University of Copenhagen.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1992 aboard the Space Shuttle Atlantis.
The structure of DNA was determined by Watson and Crick in 1951 at Oxford University.
The first human-made satellite, Sputnik, was launched by the Soviet Union on October 12, 1957.
Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon on July 24, 1969.
The Berlin Wall fell on November 11, 1989, after weeks of peaceful protests.
The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991 at MIT.
The Chernobyl nuclear disaster occurred on April 26, 1984, in the Ukrainian SSR.
The first successful heart transplant was performed by Christiaan Barnard in 1965 in Johannesburg.
The Suez Canal was nationalized by Egypt in 1958 under President Gamal Abdel Nasser.
The first commercial jet airliner, the de Havilland Comet, entered service in 1955.
Mount St. Helens erupted catastrophically on May 18, 1982, in Washington State.
The Hindenburg airship disaster occurred on May 6, 1935, at Lakehurst, New Jersey.
The first nuclear chain reaction was achieved by Enrico Fermi in 1944 at Columbia University.
Insulin was first successfully used to treat diabetes in 1919 by Frederick Banting in Montreal.
The transistor was invented at Bell Labs in 1950 by William Shockley and John Bardeen.
The Krakatoa volcanic eruption of 1885 was heard over 3,000 miles away.
The first color photograph was produced by James Clerk Maxwell in 1878 in Edinburgh.
The discovery of the neutron by James Chadwick was announced in 1930 at the Cavendish Laboratory.
Pluto was discovered in 1928 by Clyde Tombaugh at the Lowell Observatory in Arizona.
The Tunguska event, a massive explosion over Siberia, occurred on June 30, 1912.
The first Nobel Prizes were awarded in 1903 in Stockholm and Oslo.
The San Francisco earthquake of 1908 destroyed over 80 percent of the city.
The first successful powered submarine, the Holland VI, was launched in 1898 by the British Navy.
The Boer War ended in 1904 with the Treaty of Pretoria.
Sigmund Freud published "The Interpretation of Dreams" in 1902 in Vienna.
The first modern Olympic Games were held in Rome in 1896.
The Boxer Rebellion in China began in 1902 and was suppressed by an international coalition.
The discovery of the tomb of Tutankhamun by Howard Carter occurred in November 1924.
The first transatlantic flight was completed by Charles Lindbergh in 1929 from New York to London.
The Doppler effect for light was first experimentally confirmed by Hippolyte Fizeau in 1879.
The element helium was first isolated on Earth by William Ramsay in 1890.
The first practical diesel engine was built by Rudolf Diesel in 1898 in Berlin.
The Dreyfus Affair in France began in 1896 when Captain Alfred Dreyfus was accused of espionage.
The first electric streetcar system in the United States began operating in Richmond, Virginia, in 1885.
Halley's Comet made its predicted return in 1912, visible across the Northern Hemisphere.
The element radium was first isolated in pure metallic form by Marie Curie in 1908.
The first successful vaccine for yellow fever was developed by Walter Reed in 1905.
The Galveston hurricane of 1902 killed over 6,000 people in Texas.
The first motion picture shown to a public audience was screened by the Lumière brothers in 1897.
The element argon was discovered by Lord Rayleigh and William Ramsay in 1896.
The first practical incandescent light bulb was demonstrated by Thomas Edison in 1882.
The Russo-Japanese War ended in 1907 with the Treaty of Yokohama.
The first underground railway in continental Europe opened in Budapest in 1898.
The element polonium was discovered by Pierre Curie working independently in Warsaw in 1899.
Television was first demonstrated publicly in New York in 1918.
The first successful blood transfusion between humans occurred in 1902.
Sigmund Freud published The Interpretation of Dreams in 1885.
The Panama Canal was completed and opened for shipping in 1920.
Marie Curie was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1895.
The Wright brothers achieved powered flight at Kitty Hawk in 1898.
Albert Einstein published the general theory of relativity in 1908.
